# Workflows

## Git Integration

### What Gets Committed

Loadout auto-manages `.gitignore` to exclude only rendered outputs:

```gitignore
# <loadout>
# Auto-generated by loadout. Do not edit this section.
.loadout/.state.json
.cursor/rules/coding-style.mdc
.claude/rules/coding-style.md
# </loadout>
```

**Committed:** `.loadout/` (source), `AGENTS.md` (canonical), `.gitignore`
**Local only:** Rendered outputs, state file

### Custom Tool Configs

Because loadout ignores specific paths (not whole directories), you can have custom configs alongside managed ones:

```
.cursor/
├── rules/
│   ├── coding-style.mdc    # Managed by loadout (ignored)
│   └── my-custom.mdc       # Your custom rule (committed)
└── mcp.json                # Tool settings (committed)
```

---

## Team Onboarding

After cloning a repo with loadout, team members need to render outputs once. Choose one approach:

### Option 1: Git Hooks (Recommended)

Run once after cloning:
```bash
git config core.hooksPath .loadout/hooks
```

This auto-syncs on `git checkout` and `git pull`.

**Caveats:**
- Must run from git root (not subprojects)
- Replaces any existing `core.hooksPath` setting
- To undo: `git config --unset core.hooksPath`

**For JS projects**, automate in `package.json`:
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "prepare": "git config core.hooksPath .loadout/hooks 2>/dev/null || true"
  }
}
```

### Option 2: Direnv

If using [direnv](https://direnv.net/):
```bash
direnv allow
```

Auto-syncs when entering the directory. Good for teams already using direnv.

### Option 3: Manual Sync

For users without loadout installed or preferring manual control:
```bash
loadout sync
```

Hooks skip gracefully if loadout isn't installed, so CI and teammates without loadout won't error.

---

## Monorepo Setup

Put shared config at repo root, package-specific in each package:

```
~/code/monorepo/
├── .loadout/                       # Repo-level
│   ├── loadouts/base.yaml          # Shared base
│   ├── rules/shared-style.md
│   └── skills/debugging/
├── packages/
│   └── api/
│       ├── .loadout/
│       │   ├── loadout.yaml        # sources: [../..]
│       │   └── loadouts/api.yaml   # extends: base
│       └── src/
```

In `packages/api/.loadout/loadout.yaml`:
```yaml
version: "1"
sources:
  - ../..                           # Points to monorepo root
```

Now `api` loadout can `extends: base` from the parent.

---

## CI/CD

Most CI pipelines don't need loadout — AI tools typically aren't used in CI.

**When to use loadout in CI:**
- Automated AI code review workflows
- Validating loadout configuration on PRs

**Example CI step:**
```bash
npm install -g @evatt/loadout
loadout check -v          # Validate config (catches broken references)
loadout sync --dry-run    # Preview what would be rendered
```

If your CI step actually needs the rendered outputs:
```bash
loadout sync              # Render outputs
```

---

## Common Workflows

### Daily Context Switching
```bash
loadout activate base backend     # Morning: backend work
loadout activate base frontend    # Afternoon: switch to frontend
loadout deactivate frontend       # Remove frontend, keep base
```

### After Editing Rules/Skills
```bash
# Edit the source file
$EDITOR .loadout/rules/my-rule.md

# Re-render to all tools
loadout sync

# Verify the change
loadout status
```

### Check for Drift
```bash
loadout status      # See what changed
loadout sync        # Reconcile
```

### Adding Global Personal Config
```bash
loadout init -g
loadout rule add -g my-style
loadout activate base -g
```

---

## Shadowed Files

A **shadowed file** is when loadout wants to write to a path that already has an unmanaged file.

Loadout **never overwrites** unmanaged files. It skips and reports in `loadout status`.

**To resolve:**
```bash
# Import the existing file
loadout rule import .cursor/rules/existing.mdc

# Or remove it manually
rm .cursor/rules/existing.mdc && loadout sync

# Or keep it (it takes precedence)
```
